raw_content:
# Comparing #hybe #sm #jyp #yg Groups Concepts & Management
## Asian Entertainment and Culture
48600 subscribers
284 likes

### Description
4101 views
Posted: 24 May 2025
Patreon:  http://patreon.com/asianEAC
Website: http://www.madsoulasylum.com
Talk to me anytime: https://twitter.com/AsianEAC
For more personal stuff: 
https://www.instagram.com/iamfairlane/
https://www.twitter.com/iamfairlane/
____________________________________________________
Dive into the world of K-pop as we explore the distinct concepts and management styles of the four major entertainment agencies: HYBE, SM Entertainment, JYP Entertainment, and YG Entertainment. Each company has carved its unique path in shaping the global K-pop phenomenon.

25 comments
### Transcript:
What keeps a K-pop group alive after the spotlight fades? Is it the fans, the music, the story, or is it the system that created them in the first place? We often talk about debut concepts, viral songs, or stage presence, but the real secret to longevity doesn't just rely on great music. A huge part of it relies on how a company treat their artist. YG, SMG, YP, and Hib built empires, but each one has a radically different blueprint for how to sustain the success. In this video, let's look at how these companies manage their artists, why some groups thrive while others fade, and what it really takes to last in an industry built on constant reinvention. [Music] First, let's talk about artist and creative development. Let's go company by company. First up, SM Entertainment. Business first, concept second. SM has led K-pop in more ways than one. Aside from being the first agency of its kind in Korea and creating the foundational K-pop system, SM has consistently launched groups with distinct concepts. While Hot was the pioneer TVXQ was crafted to look and sound like a Japanese boy band. Super Jr. was the first group with that many members. XO was a group with a sci-fi from outer space concept and ESPA was introduced as existing in a parallel metaverse. This concepts led to iconic releases like myotic, sorry, story and black mamba. This unique ideas are primarily driven by marketing insights and business opportunities. Founder and former CEO Suman often analyze the cultural and consumer landscape identifying trends, needs, and styles that the younger generations are likely to engage with or markets the company can tap into. Super Junior, for example, had many members to allow for broad participation in variety shows, endorsements, TV dramas, and more. That gives them more ways to earn. ESPA was built to appeal to a generation immersed in gaming and virtual reality. SM starts with a business or a marketing opportunity first and then builds the creative direction around that. For Hib, it's story first, marketing follows. Hib is the complete opposite of SM. The concept and marketing of their groups begin with the creative narrative. BTS even before they had a name began with a genre. Bangshi wanted to form a hip-hop group. As the members came together, they pushed to tell their own stories instead of relying on outsourced songs. Leraphim was founded on the concept of fearlessness, building music that lives on the edge. Ilet plays with dual identities in dual worlds and Hypen leans into a darker, more edgy aesthetics. The genre and story of the group shape how they are promoted and to a large extent the music that they create. BTS has developed an epic narrative that spans their entire career with each song progressing the story while also sharing their personal experiences with youth, growth, fame, struggle and eventually peace. Leraphim started with themes of boldness and strength, then moved into exploring hardships and pain, ultimately arriving at a message. Courage doesn't mean the absence of fear, but the act of pushing through it. Both the sound and visual direction of these groups evolve alongside the narrative arc. Y Entertainment, if it ain't broke, amplify it. Yang Yun Suk aimed to rise above the clutter of mainstream K-pop. Drawing from the style and legacy of Sautaian Boys, his former group, he created a hiphop centered idol group, then packaged them as artists, not just as idols. He reinforced this with high impact visuals and big budget music videos. Swagger was part of the brand from the beginning. While other idols were ubiquitous, Yang carefully curated where Big Bang and eventually 21 would appear. Their scarcity became a part of their mystique. They weren't everywhere because they weren't meant to be. Though he hasn't fully recaptured the artist dynamic of Big Bang and 21, he has successfully replicated the rest. Groups like Winner, Icon, and Blackpink have long hiatuses between releases. Each comeback is minimal in output, usually just a mini album. What remains consistent is WG's visual signature. Everything looks massive, shiny, and expensive. The new group, Baby Monster, follows the same formula. Same sound, same concept, same approach to marketing. YG found a formula that works, and as long as the market keeps asking for it, they see no reason to change it. JYPp excellence not uniqueness. JYPp thrives by refining existing concepts rather than inventing entirely new ones. Focusing on execution over novelty, they form groups that perform proven ideas exceptionally well. Twice, for example, is the quintessential Korean girl group. Soft, cheerful, and relatable. Etsy filled a gap in the market by becoming a performance focused girl group when no one else was doing it at that time. Stray Kids may be the exception. They have more flexible and experimental concept because they are in full control over their creatives. So in summary, SM treats concept as a marketing solution. Hib treats concepts as the story engine. YG scales one proven brand philosophy. NJP masters execution with familiar formats. Let's talk about how the agency sustain commercial success. And first up, YG. Their success is almost always shadowed by scandal. Y has been consistent in its exclusive and infrequent approach to sustaining its idol groups. Comebacks after the initial seven-year contract mark are often even rarer. However, the challenge often isn't commercial, it's legal. Many YG idols have encountered legal issues that disrupt group activities and make it difficult to plan returns. In Korea, it is very common for idols to go on a hiatus following a scandal, and YG has had several. Others have left due to contract disputes. Two members of Big Bang were convicted of crimes. Mino of Winner is currently facing accusations related to his military service. Nam Teeun left after a creative difference with YG. BI departed ICON following drug allegations which were eventually proven false. 21 disbanded at least temporarily amid contract issues involving Minzy and controversies surrounding Park Bomb. So far, Blackpink is the only group that has managed to avoid major public issues. However, each member has pursued their own solo careers while maintaining a group contract with YG. They are scheduled to do the world tour this coming summer. Next up, SM Entertainment. Marketdriven longevity, but only to a point. SM Entertainment has launched some of the most successful acts in K-pop history. From Boa to TVXQ to Girls Generation to Super Junior to EXO, much of their success comes from an ability to anticipate and respond to market demands. The downside is that SM's model reflects the volatility of the market it serves, short attention spans, and youthdriven consumer base. K-pop is largely an industry off and for the young and youth doesn't last forever. In fact, it's pretty short. That coupled with the standard 7-year contract, after which more leverage shifts to the artists, makes it more practical for SM to invest in a new group than to sustain older ones with the same intensity or capital. That's not to say SM has never had longlasting acts. Artists like Temen and Ton have continued strong solo careers, but overall solo careers tend to thrive more than groups after the 7-year mark. Hib group identity first longevity follows. Hib has always placed strong emphasis on group activities. While they've supported individual pursuits as evidenced in their early backing of SM Shuga and JH Hope solo projects, the focus remains in the group identity. Official solo careers tend to be launched only after the group's brand and narrative are firmly established. This may be one reason why Hib has managed to sustain group activities and success beyond the typical 7-year window. That said, Hib's track record is still relatively short. They are a pretty young company. Only three groups, BTS, TXD, and 17 have reached the point where long-term sustainability can be measured. TXT and 17 have both renew their contracts. 17's upcoming enlistment will most likely result in a hiatus, and we will see if the members will actually coordinate their service to shorten the time of their group hiatus. TXT meanwhile has remained active with each member beginning to release solo music. As for BTS, they've gone as far as to synchronize their enlistment to ensure group activities can resume together. But prior to enlistment, as we all know, each member has launched their solo careers and each member has thrived both locally and internationally. JYPp sustainability through character and cohesion. JYPp one generation younger than SM appears to have learned from earlier models and has improved in its ability to sustain groups post seven-year contracts. While 2PM, Wonder Girls, and GOT7 ceased regular group activities after their initial terms, none of them actually officially disbanded and some of them actually made a comeback. members have continued to speak positively of the company as well and in some cases returned for reunions or collaborative projects. This suggests that JYP prioritizes maintaining long-term relationships over controlling every phase of an artist's career. Look at Twice. They remain active 10 years into their career and is preparing for what may be their biggest world tour yet. An unusual feat for a girl group, especially one that debuted with a youthful concept. Their evolving sound, growing creative input, and consistent team dynamic have allowed them to mature alongside their audience. Stray Kids is also entering stadium tour territory, having built a strong global fan base through self-produced music and creative autonomy from early on. JYPp has often said that character is key in idol selection that values must align and it's that focus that may be the reason so many of their artists maintain goodwill with the company even after moving on it's not just a business model it's a culture of trust that's proving to be sustainable creative evolution truth be told has come up with some of K-pop's most ambitious concepts But the challenge isn't launching them. It's keeping them coherent and evolving them. XO debuted with elemental superpowers and parallel units meant to conquer both Korea and China and other parts of the world. It was bold, cinematic, and full of potential. Until it wasn't. The superpower concept faded from performances and music videos over time, becoming more of a footnote than a foundation. The same pattern followed with ESPA. Their metaverse inspired dual avatars and AI lore were ahead of the curve, but the storytelling fizzled, often overshadowed by disconnected comebacks and unclear execution. SM can dream big, but translating those dreams into lasting narratives that fans can follow and feel is where things tend to fall apart. JYPp on the other hand knows how to grow with its artists. Twice started as the ultimate girl nextoor group, bright, bubbly, and youthful. But as the members matured, so did the music. Songs like feel special and talk that talk reflect deeper themes. And the group now has a hand in their own creative process. 2 p.m. walked a similar path, transforming from the wild acrobat idol group to charismatic adult idols with full control over their own artistry. Stray Kids, though, flipped the script entirely. They've self-produced since debuted, building a gritty, genre blending sound that's uniquely theirs. What JYP seems to understand is this. Giving artists room to evolve and letting them steer creatively isn't just respectful, it is also sustainable. YG, meanwhile, has stuck to its winning formula and stock hard. Teddy, their go-to producer for nearly every major group, created a signature sound that's heavy, high energy, and instantly recognizable. But over time, that sound has become predictable. Whether it's Blackpink, icon, Baby Monster, the beats, drops, and vocal arrangements often feel recycled. Visually, the music videos remain luxurious and explosive, but conceptually there's little evolution. Y's approach has been consistency over reinvention which works until it doesn't. Hi operates in a different ecosystem entirely. Worldb building is the strategy. BTS didn't just release albums. They released chapters of a long emotionally resonant story. From school days and societal pressure to self-love and existential growth, their discoraphy is a continuous narrative arc. Lesseraphim's fearless concept evolves with every comeback, reflecting internal struggles and triumphs. Even newer groups like it are given thematic blueprints that allows sonic and visual growth. At hib, music is not just a product. It's a storytelling device and that keeps fans invested long term. So, who is going to dominate in the future? Well, SM is standing at a major crossroads and this time there's no Yumu man to point the way with its founder and long-term creative visionary out of the picture. The shift is already showing. SM's current leadership is stacked with businesses and marketing minds. But what they lack is a creative north star. Someone who can steer bold ideas into cultural movements, not just product launches. SM has always been the blueprint maker, the label that invented a K-pop system. The real question now is, can they still lead a creative revolution or are they about to become the luxury mall of K-pop? slick, structured, and soulless. YG, on the other hand, seems to be evolving, but maybe not by choice. Their elite exclusive aura remains intact, but the playbook is shifting. They finally are working with producers outside of Teddy, and Baby Monster is being sent to shows and overseas stores much earlier than Blackpink ever was. It's a subtle but clear shift. YG seems to be moving from you'll wait for us to we'll meet you halfway the only missing piece brand prestige Blackpink bagged luxury campaigns practically at debut baby monster despite the buzz hasn't landed that level of commercial cache yet whether all this hype converts into profit or just noise remains to be seen JYPp is experimenting ending, but still searching for its next big wave. Twice and Stray Kids are setting the bar skyhigh with world tours and global fandoms. But newer groups haven't yet hit that level. The company is clearly trying new angles, launching a global girl group, Vicha, to tap into the western market and introducing Kickflip, a skateboarding themed boy group to ride me niche subcultures. It's ambitious and refreshing, but still in the early innings. The challenge isn't just creating new groups. It's creating groups that redefine the market like their predecessors did. Hib continues to play in a different league entirely, building fullon universes instead of just groups. Every act under the umbrella is given a narrative, a mythos, a long act of character development. But BTS's generational saga to TXT's dreams versus reality duality. Hib isn't selling idols. They're selling stories. And as long as they can keep telling compelling ones, there's no ceiling to what they can build. But here's the catch. None of that will matter if they don't evolve with times. Technology is reshaping not just how we consume music, but what music is. AI generated art, virtual idols, and fan created content are becoming the norm. The battle isn't just for chart positions anymore. It's for relevance in an ever fracturing attention economy. SM YG, JYPp, and Hive each built the foundation of modern K-pop. But to survive the next era, they'll have to break their own minds and their own molds. Because the next generation of fans won't just stream songs. They'll help define the sound. The challenge may be not just for smyg to reinvent themselves but to actually reinvent what music is for the next generation. I would love to know to know your thoughts and um pick your brain as to where you think this is headed. Not just K-pop but just the music industry in general. There's I I think for once in our history, we finally got to not finally, but we got to that point where it's just so hard to see the future because of all these moving parts. And somehow I feel like the direction that this the different directions that this major music labels in Korea are taking may actually represent the different possibilities that where music could actually be in 10, 20, 30 years. I would love to hear your thoughts. So leave them in the comment section below. As always, you can say whatever it is that you want to say. Just please do so respectfully. Also, please follow us on X and also on Instagram. But more importantly, please follow us on Patreon. It's a vibrant community. We have a lot of discussion, not just on BTS, not just on K-pop, but in music in general, mu movies, TV shows, and sometimes we just have fun. And we do have a free tier just in case you want to try things out first. Thank you. I'll see you there. [Music]

content:
# 하이브 SM JYP YG, 같은 엔터사가 아니다: 재무제표가 말하는 진짜 차이
## 유니콘정글
10800 subscribers
9 likes

### Description
601 views
Posted: 23 May 2026
하이브·SM·JYP·YG는 모두 K팝 대표 엔터사로 묶입니다.  
하지만 2025년 사업보고서를 열어보면, 네 회사는 전혀 다른 방식으로 돈을 벌고 있습니다.

하이브는 매출 규모 1위지만 수익성 부담이 컸고,  
SM은 영업이익 1위를 기록했으며,  
JYP는 가장 높은 이익률을 보여줬고,  
YG는 적자에서 흑자로 돌아섰습니다.

이번 영상에서는 2025년 연결 재무제표를 기준으로 K팝 4대 엔터사의 매출, 영업이익, 순손익, 사업 구조를 비교해 봅니다.

핵심 질문은 이것입니다.

“엔터사의 진짜 경쟁력은 아티스트 인기일까, 아니면 그 인기를 반복 매출과 이익으로 바꾸는 사업 구조일까?”

유니콘정글은 스타트업, AI, 투자, 조직 성장을 쉽게 해설하는 테크·스타트업 미디어입니다. 창업자와 실무자를 위한 기업 분석, 리더십, 재무, IR, 기술 트렌드 콘텐츠를 만듭니다.

구독·좋아요로 ‘유니콘정글’과 함께 성장해 보세요!

#유니콘정글 #하이브 #SM #JYP #YG #K팝 #엔터주 #엔터테인먼트 #기업분석 #재무제표분석 #사업보고서 #투자공부 #스타트업 #창업 #비즈니스 [...] 효율적인 회사입니다. YG는 대형 IP의 폭발적인 회복력을 기대해 볼 수 있는 반전의 매력이 있습니다. K팝 회사들은 겉으로는 비슷해 보여도 돈을 버는 방식은 완전히 다르다는 것을 알 수 있었습니다. [음악] 하이브는 규모, SM은 이익, JYP는 효율, 그리고 YG는 반전이라는 각자의 키워드를 가지고 있었죠. [음악] 지금까지 시청해 주셔서 감사드립니다. 유니콘 정글 구독 잊지 마시고요. 그럼 안녕. H [...] 있습니다. 소수 대형 IP의 폭발력과 변동성을 [음악] 동시에 가진 터너라운드 기업이라고 할 수 있겠네요. 이제 K팝 44의 포지션을 한 눈에 정리해 볼까요? 파이브는 글로벌 IP 플랫폼 그룹으로 규모를 확장하고 있습니다. SM은 IP 포트폴리오 체질 개선을 통해 이익을 극대화하고 있고요. [음악] JYP는 고수익 운영형 엔터사로 효율을 가장 중요하게 생각합니다. 마지막으로 YG는 소수 대형 IP의 터너라운드를 통해 반전을 노리고 있습니다. [음악] 각 회사마다 정말 다른 전략을 가지고 있다는 것을 알 수 있네요. 네. 각 회사의 전략이 정말 다르다는 걸 알 수 있었는데요. 그렇다면 지금부터는 각 회사 앞에 놓인 핵심 과제들을 하나씩 짚어 볼까요? 하이브는 거대한 투자 비용이 언제쯤 이익으로 돌아올지가 가장 큰 질문입니다. SM은 체질 개선 이후에 어떤 새로운 성장 동력을 찾아낼지가 중요하고요. JYP는 효율성만으로 어 [음악] 지금의 규모를 계속 확장할 수 있을지가 관건입니다. 어 마지막으로 YG는 대형 IP 리스크를 어떻게 관리할지가 핵심 과제라고 할 수 있습니다. 이렇게 각 회사마다 풀어야 할 숙제가 명확하게 보이네요. 네. 시청자분들께 질문을 드려 볼 시간입니다. 여러분이라면이 내 회사 중에 어디에 투자하시겠습니까? [음악] 하이브는 거대한 규모와 미래를 위한 큰 그림을 가지고 있습니다. SM은 이미 증명된 IP 수익화 능력이 강점이고요. [음악] JYP는 본업에서 가장 선명한 수익성을 보여주는 효율적인 회사입니다. YG는 대형 IP의 폭발적인 회복력을 기대해 볼 수 있는 반전의 매력이 있습니다.

### Transcript:
여러분, K파 엔터사들이 다 같은 회사라고 생각하셨나요? 오늘 저희가 그 고정관념을 확 깨드리겠습니다. 네, 오늘 저희가팝 사사에 숨겨진 이야기를 숫자로 명확하게 보여 드릴 겁니다. 오늘은 특별히 다섯 가지 핵심 포인트를 짚어 볼 거예요. 각 회사의 재무 상태를 깊이 있게 들여다보면서 왜 이런 차이가 발생하는지 분석해 드릴게요. 그럼 본격적으로 시작해 볼까요? 모니폭스 님, 방금 말씀하신 것처럼 매출 1위 회사의 영업 이익이 사중에 꼴찌라는게 정말 믿기지 않네요. 저만 놀란 건 아니겠죠? 도대체 어떤 이유 때문에 이런 결과가 나오는 건지 너무 궁금합니다. [음악] 네. 바로이 표가 그 질문에 대한 답을 담고 있습니다. 매출만으로는 회사의 진짜 가치를 알 수 없어요.이 표를 보시면 K팝 44의 2025년 핵심 지표들이 한 눈에 [음악] 들어오실 겁니다. 매출, 영업 이익, 영업 이익률, 그리고 단기 순손위까지 [음악] 각 회사의 재무 상태를 명확하게 비교할 수 있죠. 특히 하이브의 영업 이익률이 다른 회사들과 크게 차이나는 것을 볼 수 있습니다. [음악] 하이브는 매출 규모는 가장 크지만 영업 이익률은 가장 낮았습니다. 하이브는 단순히 음반만 파는 회사가 아니에요. 음악, 플랫폼, 그리고 테크를 아우르는 글로벌 IP 플랫폼 그룹을 지향하고 있죠. [음악] 하지만 이런 큰 그림을 그리는 과정에서 막대한 선재 투자가 필요했습니다. 신규 아티스트 대비 초기 비용이나 북미 사업 구조 개편 과정에서 발생한 일회성 비용들이 현재 이익을 압박하고 있는 거죠. 하이브에이어서 SM은 어떤 전략으로 움직이고 있나요? [음악] SM은 오래된 IP와 새로운 IP를 동시에 활용해서 영업이 1위를 달성했다고 하네요. 2025년에는 연결 매출이 1조 1749억 원, 영업 이익은 [음악] 18,830억 원으로 역대 최대 성과를 낼 것으로 예상됩니다. 기존 아티스트의 안정적인 활동과 신규 [음악] 아티스트의 빠른 성장이 시너지를 내고 있어요. 여기에 글로벌 콘서트 투어와 MD 확대 그리고 D어유 연결 편입 효과까지 더해져서 수익성이 크게 개선될 것으로 보입니다. 와, 머니폭스님, JYP는 효율의 정석이라고 불리네요. [음악] 2025년 영업 이익률이 18.9%로 9%로 K팝 44 중 압도적인 1위라고 합니다. 하이브처럼 대규모 플랫폼 투자 없이 본업의 수익성을 극대화하는 전략이 정말 인상깊어요. [음악] 아티스트 운영, 음반, 공연, 매니지먼트 수익화를 가장 효율적으로 해내고 있어요. [음악] 가장 큰 회사는 아니지만 본업에서 돈을 남기는 능력이 가장 선명하다는 점이 JYP의 핵심 강점입니다. [음악] 와지는 대형 IP가 움직이면 실적이 빠르게 살아난다고 하네요. 25년에는 연결 매출 5,454억 원, 영업 이익 713억 원으로 2024년 적자에서 흑자 전환이 예상됩니다. 소수 대형 IP의 활동 재개시 폭발적인 실적 회복력을 보여주는 것이 장점이에요. 하지만 대형 IP 활동 공백 시에는 실적 변동성이 높다는 리스크도 함께 가지고 있습니다. 소수 대형 IP의 폭발력과 변동성을 [음악] 동시에 가진 터너라운드 기업이라고 할 수 있겠네요. 이제 K팝 44의 포지션을 한 눈에 정리해 볼까요? 파이브는 글로벌 IP 플랫폼 그룹으로 규모를 확장하고 있습니다. SM은 IP 포트폴리오 체질 개선을 통해 이익을 극대화하고 있고요. [음악] JYP는 고수익 운영형 엔터사로 효율을 가장 중요하게 생각합니다. 마지막으로 YG는 소수 대형 IP의 터너라운드를 통해 반전을 노리고 있습니다. [음악] 각 회사마다 정말 다른 전략을 가지고 있다는 것을 알 수 있네요. 네. 각 회사의 전략이 정말 다르다는 걸 알 수 있었는데요. 그렇다면 지금부터는 각 회사 앞에 놓인 핵심 과제들을 하나씩 짚어 볼까요? 하이브는 거대한 투자 비용이 언제쯤 이익으로 돌아올지가 가장 큰 질문입니다. SM은 체질 개선 이후에 어떤 새로운 성장 동력을 찾아낼지가 중요하고요. JYP는 효율성만으로 어 [음악] 지금의 규모를 계속 확장할 수 있을지가 관건입니다. 어 마지막으로 YG는 대형 IP 리스크를 어떻게 관리할지가 핵심 과제라고 할 수 있습니다. 이렇게 각 회사마다 풀어야 할 숙제가 명확하게 보이네요. 네. 시청자분들께 질문을 드려 볼 시간입니다. 여러분이라면이 내 회사 중에 어디에 투자하시겠습니까? [음악] 하이브는 거대한 규모와 미래를 위한 큰 그림을 가지고 있습니다. SM은 이미 증명된 IP 수익화 능력이 강점이고요. [음악] JYP는 본업에서 가장 선명한 수익성을 보여주는 효율적인 회사입니다. YG는 대형 IP의 폭발적인 회복력을 기대해 볼 수 있는 반전의 매력이 있습니다. K팝 회사들은 겉으로는 비슷해 보여도 돈을 버는 방식은 완전히 다르다는 것을 알 수 있었습니다. [음악] 하이브는 규모, SM은 이익, JYP는 효율, 그리고 YG는 반전이라는 각자의 키워드를 가지고 있었죠. [음악] 지금까지 시청해 주셔서 감사드립니다. 유니콘 정글 구독 잊지 마시고요. 그럼 안녕. H

title: SM과 YG, JYP와 하이브는 어떻게 다를까 - 톱클래스
content:
4) 트렌디한 사운드

최신 팝 트렌드를 빠르게 흡수하여 세련되고 고급스러운 사운드를 만들어냅니다.

### 

### 3. JYP 엔터테인먼트: "대중성, 그루브, 그리고 건강한 에너지"

JYP는 전통적으로 대중이 따라 부르기 쉽고 즐기기 좋은 음악을 선호합니다.

JYP의 걸그룹 ITZY 

JYP의 걸그룹 ITZY

1) 리듬과 그루브

수장인 박진영의 영향으로 흑인 음악(소울, 펑크, 디스코) 기반의 리듬감을 중요시합니다. '공기 반 소리 반'으로 대변되는 자연스러운 창법을 선호합니다.

2) 걸그룹 명가

원더걸스-트와이스-있지(ITZY)로 이어지는 라인업은 대중적이고 캐치한 멜로디, 건강하고 밝은 에너지를 강조합니다.

3) 자체 프로듀싱(스트레이 키즈)

최근에는 회사 주도형 기획에서 벗어나, 스트레이 키즈처럼 멤버들이 직접 작사·작곡하는 '마라맛(강렬하고 자극적인)' 음악으로 글로벌 팬덤을 확장했습니다.

4) 믹스 팝(엔믹스):엔

믹스(NMIXX)를 통해 두 가지 이상의 장르를 섞는 실험적인 '믹스 팝'을 시도하기도 했습니다.

### 

### 4. 하이브(HYBE): "세련된 팝, 서사(Storytelling), 멀티 레이블"

하이브는 단일 색깔보다는 산하 레이블(빅히트, 플레디스, 어도어, 쏘스뮤직 등)마다 독자적인 색깔을 유지하는 것이 특징입니다.

하이브의 걸그룹 르세라핌 

하이브의 걸그룹 르세라핌

1) 빅히트 (방탄소년단, 투모로우바이투게더) [...] 하이브의 걸그룹 르세라핌 

하이브의 걸그룹 르세라핌

1) 빅히트 (방탄소년단, 투모로우바이투게더)

멤버들의 자전적인 이야기와 서사(세계관)를 중요시합니다. 힙합을 베이스로 하되, 서구권 팝 시장에 어울리는 세련된 사운드를 구현합니다.

2) 어도어 (뉴진스)

민희진 전 대표의 기획으로 시작된 Y2K 감성과 '이지 리스닝' 트렌드를 주도했습니다. 힘을 뺀 자연스러움과 몽환적인 팝 사운드가 특징입니다.

3) 쏘스뮤직 (르세라핌)

세련된 런웨이 음악, 펑크(Funk)나 라틴 팝 등 패션과 음악을 결합한 듯한 시크하고 강렬한 컨셉을 보여줍니다.

4) 전반적 특징

높은 자본력을 바탕으로 곡의 퀄리티(특히 비트와 믹싱)가 매우 높고, 서구권 팝 트렌드를 가장 발 빠르게 반영합니다.

최근(2023~2024년)에는 전 세계적으로 '이지 리스닝(Easy Listening, 듣기 편한 음악)'이 유행하면서, 대부분의 기획사가 공통적으로 힘을 꽉 준 퍼포먼스형 음악보다는 멜로디가 좋고 반복해서 듣기 좋은 음악을 내놓는 추세로 수렴하고 있기도 합니다.

저작권자 © 톱클래스 무단전재 및 재배포 금지 [...] ## 상단영역

## 본문영역

안용범 교수의 뮤직 핫플 대중음악 속 숨겨진 이야기와 깊이 있는 해설을 통해 음악 팬들의 지적 호기심을 자극한다. 다양한 음악 트렌드를 분석하고, 뮤지션들의 비하인드 스토리를 흥미롭게 풀어내어 듣는 재미를 더한다. 현재 백석예술대학교 음악학부 부교수로 재직 중이다.

### SM과 YG, JYP와 하이브는 어떻게 다를까

 입력 2026.03.03 10:37

글자 크기

프린트

안용범 다른기사 보기

#### 주요 엔터테인먼트 회사들의 고유한 음악적 색깔

대한민국의 주요 엔터테인먼트 회사들은 각자 고유한 음악적 색깔(House Style)과 기획 방향성을 가지고 있습니다. 이른바 '4대 기획사(하이브, SM, JYP, YG)'를 중심으로  음악적 컨셉과 특징을 알아볼까요?

### 1. SM 엔터테인먼트: "실험정신, 네오(Neo), 그리고 보컬"

SM은 K-팝의 정석을 만들었으면서도, 동시에 가장 실험적이고 미래지향적인 음악을 추구합니다.

SM엔터테인먼트의 걸그룹 에스파  

SM엔터테인먼트의 걸그룹 에스파

1) SMP (SM Music Performance)

사회 비판적 가사와 웅장한 퍼포먼스를 결합한 전통적인 SM 스타일입니다.(예: 동방신기, 엑소 초기)

2) 실험과 믹스

## 본문영역

![](https://cdn.topclass.chosun.com/image/box/logo-topp.gif)

**안용범 교수의 뮤직 핫플** 대중음악 속 숨겨진 이야기와 깊이 있는 해설을 통해 음악 팬들의 지적 호기심을 자극한다. 다양한 음악 트렌드를 분석하고, 뮤지션들의 비하인드 스토리를 흥미롭게 풀어내어 듣는 재미를 더한다. 현재 백석예술대학교 음악학부 부교수로 재직 중이다.

### SM과 YG, JYP와 하이브는 어떻게 다를까

* 입력 2026.03.03 10:37

글자 크기

프린트

**안용범** [다른기사 보기](/news/articleList.html?sc_area=I&sc_word=topp00152&view_type=sm)

#### 주요 엔터테인먼트 회사들의 고유한 음악적 색깔

대한민국의 주요 엔터테인먼트 회사들은 각자 고유한 음악적 색깔(House Style)과 기획 방향성을 가지고 있습니다. 이른바 '4대 기획사(하이브, SM, JYP, YG)'를 중심으로  음악적 컨셉과 특징을 알아볼까요?

---

### **1. SM 엔터테인먼트: "실험정신, 네오(Neo), 그리고 보컬"**

**SM은 K-팝의 정석을 만들었으면서도, 동시에 가장 실험적이고 미래지향적인 음악을 추구합니다.**

![SM엔터테인먼트의 걸그룹 에스파 ](https://cdn.topclass.chosun.com/news/photo/202603/35924_52164_1324.jpg) 

SM엔터테인먼트의 걸그룹 에스파

**1) SMP (SM Music Performance)**

사회 비판적 가사와 웅장한 퍼포먼스를 결합한 전통적인 SM 스타일입니다.(예: 동방신기, 엑소 초기)

**2) 실험과 믹스**

한 곡 안에 여러 장르를 섞거나 난해한 비트를 사용하는 것을 두려워하지 않습니다. 이를 '네오(Neo)하다'고 표현하기도 합니다. (예: NCT의 노래들, 에스파의 'Next Level')

**3) 보컬 하모니 중시**

댄스 음악이라도 멤버들의 화음과 보컬 실력을 매우 중요하게 생각합니다. 탄탄한 보컬 라인이 곡의 중심을 잡습니다.

**4) 세계관(Lore)**

'광야(KWANGYA)'로 대표되는 독자적인 스토리텔링을 음악에 녹여냅니다.

**5) 최근 변화**

라이즈(RIIZE)의 '이모셔널 팝'처럼 대중적이고 듣기 편한 이지 리스닝 계열도 적극적으로 시도하며 스펙트럼을 넓히고 있습니다.

### 

### **2. YG 엔터테인먼트: "힙합 베이스, 스웨그(Swag), 파티 분위기"**

YG는 설립 초기부터 **힙합과 R&B**를 기반으로 한 강렬한 음악을 추구해 왔습니다.

![YG의 걸그룹 블랙핑크 ](https://cdn.topclass.chosun.com/news/photo/202603/35924_52165_1423.jpg) 

YG의 걸그룹 블랙핑크

**1) 힙합 아이덴티티**

아이돌 음악이지만 힙합 비트가 기본 베이스입니다. 래퍼 라인의 비중이 크고 딕션(발음)과 플로우가 뚜렷합니다.

**2) 테디(Teddy) 스타일**

'빌드업 → 드롭(Drop)'으로 이어지는 강렬한 후렴구 구조가 특징입니다. 복잡한 화성보다는 귀에 꽂히는 킬링 파트와 비트를 중시합니다. (예: 블랙핑크, 베이비몬스터)

**3) 무대 장악력**

'칼군무'보다는 무대 위에서 멤버 개개인의 매력과 스웨그(Swag)를 뽐내며 관객과 함께 노는 파티 분위기를 지향합니다.

**4) 트렌디한 사운드**

최신 팝 트렌드를 빠르게 흡수하여 세련되고 고급스러운 사운드를 만들어냅니다.

### 

### **3. JYP 엔터테인먼트: "대중성, 그루브, 그리고 건강한 에너지"**

JYP는 전통적으로 **대중이 따라 부르기 쉽고 즐기기 좋은 음악**을 선호합니다.

![JYP의 걸그룹 ITZY](https://cdn.topclass.chosun.com/news/photo/202603/35924_52166_1522.jpg) 

JYP의 걸그룹 ITZY

**1) 리듬과 그루브**

수장인 박진영의 영향으로 흑인 음악(소울, 펑크, 디스코) 기반의 리듬감을 중요시합니다. '공기 반 소리 반'으로 대변되는 자연스러운 창법을 선호합니다.

**2) 걸그룹 명가**

원더걸스-트와이스-있지(ITZY)로 이어지는 라인업은 대중적이고 캐치한 멜로디, 건강하고 밝은 에너지를 강조합니다.

**3) 자체 프로듀싱(스트레이 키즈)**

최근에는 회사 주도형 기획에서 벗어나, 스트레이 키즈처럼 멤버들이 직접 작사·작곡하는 '마라맛(강렬하고 자극적인)' 음악으로 글로벌 팬덤을 확장했습니다.

**4) 믹스 팝(엔믹스):**엔

믹스(NMIXX)를 통해 두 가지 이상의 장르를 섞는 실험적인 '믹스 팝'을 시도하기도 했습니다.

### 

### **4. 하이브(HYBE): "세련된 팝, 서사(Storytelling), 멀티 레이블"**

하이브는 단일 색깔보다는 산하 레이블(빅히트, 플레디스, 어도어, 쏘스뮤직 등)마다 **독자적인 색깔**을 유지하는 것이 특징입니다.

![하이브의 걸그룹 르세라핌](https://cdn.topclass.chosun.com/news/photo/202603/35924_52168_1644.jpg) 

하이브의 걸그룹 르세라핌

**1) 빅히트 (방탄소년단, 투모로우바이투게더)**

멤버들의 자전적인 이야기와 서사(세계관)를 중요시합니다. 힙합을 베이스로 하되, 서구권 팝 시장에 어울리는 세련된 사운드를 구현합니다.

**2) 어도어 (뉴진스)**

민희진 전 대표의 기획으로 시작된 Y2K 감성과 '이지 리스닝' 트렌드를 주도했습니다. 힘을 뺀 자연스러움과 몽환적인 팝 사운드가 특징입니다.

**3) 쏘스뮤직 (르세라핌)**

세련된 런웨이 음악, 펑크(Funk)나 라틴 팝 등 패션과 음악을 결합한 듯한 시크하고 강렬한 컨셉을 보여줍니다.

**4) 전반적 특징**

높은 자본력을 바탕으로 곡의 퀄리티(특히 비트와 믹싱)가 매우 높고, 서구권 팝 트렌드를 가장 발 빠르게 반영합니다.

최근(2023~2024년)에는 전 세계적으로 '이지 리스닝(Easy Listening, 듣기 편한 음악)'이 유행하면서, 대부분의 기획사가 공통적으로 힘을 꽉 준 퍼포먼스형 음악보다는 멜로디가 좋고 반복해서 듣기 좋은 음악을 내놓는 추세로 수렴하고 있기도 합니다.

저작권자 © 톱클래스 무단전재 및 재배포 금지

title: Your idols' style by agency - The Korea Herald
content:
Hybe: Diverse styles under one roof

It is challenging to name one style for Hybe due to its multilabel system encompassing seven subsidiaries, each with its own distinct sound. Labels like Pledis Entertainment, Belift Lab and Ador, represented by popular K-pop groups Seventeen, Enhypen and NewJeans, respectively, each bring unique musical elements together under Hybe’s umbrella.

Formerly Big Hit Entertainment, Hybe’s identity initially revolved around BTS, the most successful K-pop act in history. Lim explained that BTS debuted with a hip-hop-inspired sound, drawing from the Billboard-topping hip-hop hits of the early 2010s.

BTS of Big Hit Music (Hybe) [...] Park is known for his philosophy of “half air, half sound” in vocal technique, prioritizing natural, relatable performances even if they’re not technically perfect. “JYP Entertainment focuses on the artist’s natural persona, aiming to connect with audiences through authenticity,” a local K-pop agency official said. “This approach gives JYP idols a relatable charm that resonates with fans at a deeper level.”

Park’s influence also extends to the agency’s focus on alluring stage presence, creating performances that highlight the unique charms of JYP groups.

YG Entertainment: Hip-hop, individuality [...] Twice of JYP Entertainment (JYP Entertainment)

JYP’s approach to group concepts also varies by gender. Boy bands like 2PM emphasized a powerful, intense style focused on hip-hop and electronic sounds with a distinctly masculine flair.

“Girl groups, meanwhile, leaned into cuteness and sensuality, often incorporating catchy hooks and approachable aesthetics. JYP girl groups tended to maintain thematic continuity across generations, with earlier concepts emphasizing traditional gender roles, a trend that shifted with newer acts like Itzy,” Lim said.
raw_content:
**K-pop* Your idols' style by agency*

# Your idols' style by agency

Published : Nov. 16, 2024 - 16:01:06

Link copied!

![Kim Jae-heun](https://wimg.heraldcorp.com/svc/user/1b869917-07ac-479e-a05a-2421fb9771fa.jpg)

SM, JYP, YG Entertainment each have distinct style reflected in their stars

![aespa of SM Entertainment (SM Entertainment)](https://wimg.heraldcorp.com/content/default/2024/11/15/20241115050548_0.jpg)

The popularity of K-pop has grown significantly in recent years, reaching a point where many around the world at least recognize the names of several groups, if not their music.

Younger audiences especially have become familiar with K-pop and can distinguish its sound from other genres. However, global K-pop fans new to the genre may struggle to recognize the unique styles of the powerhouse agencies behind some of the biggest groups.

For fans of the genre with an appreciation of its history, however, it is well known that the scene was once dominated by the “Big Three” agencies: SM Entertainment, JYP Entertainment and YG Entertainment. And each was known for a distinct musical style and unique approach to idol training, vocal delivery and visual presentation.

**SM Entertainment: Pioneering, visually driven**

Founded by Lee Soo-man, SM Entertainment is often credited with pioneering the K-pop idol training system. Lee, a tech-savvy figure, influenced the agency’s artistic and business approach, fostering groups like NCT, EXO and aespa.

![NCT Wish of SM Entertainment (SM Entertainment)](https://wimg.heraldcorp.com/content/default/2024/11/15/20241115050549_0.jpg)

Unlike JYP and YG, which depended on star producers like Bang Si-hyuk and Teddy, respectively, SM developed a sophisticated "artists and repertoire" -- or A&R -- system to source songs for each act, contributing to a unique and polished sound. Despite having in-house songwriters such as Kenzie and Yoo Young-jin, SM sought international talent early on.

For instance, 1998's “Dreams Come True” for girl group S.E.S. was written by Finnish producer Risto Asikainen, and BoA’s early hit “No. 1” was composed by Norwegian songwriter Ziggy. This global collaboration helped shape the distinctive, progressive sound that SM artists embody today.

“SM Entertainment’s style, particularly for girl groups like f(x) and Red Velvet, blends elements of Eurodance pop with unique K-pop qualities, sometimes incorporating rock or rap for a dynamic, unconventional progression,” said music critic Lim Hee-yun.

How the songs are finally selected also reflects Lee’s tastes, resulting in a cohesive musical identity that resonates with the agency’s fan base, affectionately known as "Pink Blood."

SM trainees are ingrained with a vocal style that emphasizes clear, strong projection, particularly in pop and electronic dance music, according to local K-pop vocal trainer So Su-lyn.

SM places a strong emphasis on visual appeal, grooming its idols for polished and refined images. SM artists, including NCT, EXO and aespa, often boast visually appealing lineups designed to evoke widespread fan admiration.

**JYP Entertainment: Natural, relatable**

Founded by singer Park Jin-young, JYP Entertainment has a history of evolving musical styles across generations of idols. In its early days, Park’s influence was significant, with many songs reflecting his blend of American soul, R&B and hip-hop with a Korean melodic touch. This was evident in groups like g.o.d, Rain, 2PM and Wonder Girls.

![Twice of JYP Entertainment (JYP Entertainment)](https://wimg.heraldcorp.com/content/default/2024/11/15/20241115050550_0.jpg)

JYP’s approach to group concepts also varies by gender. Boy bands like 2PM emphasized a powerful, intense style focused on hip-hop and electronic sounds with a distinctly masculine flair.

“Girl groups, meanwhile, leaned into cuteness and sensuality, often incorporating catchy hooks and approachable aesthetics. JYP girl groups tended to maintain thematic continuity across generations, with earlier concepts emphasizing traditional gender roles, a trend that shifted with newer acts like Itzy,” Lim said.

Park is known for his philosophy of “half air, half sound” in vocal technique, prioritizing natural, relatable performances even if they’re not technically perfect. “JYP Entertainment focuses on the artist’s natural persona, aiming to connect with audiences through authenticity,” a local K-pop agency official said. “This approach gives JYP idols a relatable charm that resonates with fans at a deeper level.”

Park’s influence also extends to the agency’s focus on alluring stage presence, creating performances that highlight the unique charms of JYP groups.

**YG Entertainment: Hip-hop, individuality**

YG Entertainment’s music style is heavily influenced by founder Yang Hyun-suk and in-house producer Teddy, both of whom are steeped in hip-hop. This style combines American hip-hop’s raw energy with local sensibilities to create a unique sound. Unlike JYP, which differentiates musical styles by gender, YG typically maintains a similar style across its roster, irrespective of gender.

![Baby Monster of YG Entertainment (YG Entertainment)](https://wimg.heraldcorp.com/content/default/2024/11/15/20241115050551_0.jpg)

Girl group 2NE1, for instance, embodied a masculine, hip-hop vibe in both lyrics and sound, not unlike YG boy bands. Big Bang, too, showcased rap-heavy hip-hop with bold electronic sounds in hits like “Lie” and “Last Farewell,” appealing across all age groups with their melodic hooks, reminiscent of classic ’90s K-pop.

“This catchy, singable chorus style has become a signature for YG acts, from 1TYM and Jinusean to Big Bang, Winner, Blackpink and newer groups like Baby Monster,” said Lim. “Of the ‘Big Three,’ YG Entertainment arguably has the strongest and most distinct musical identity.”

**Hybe: Diverse styles under one roof**

It is challenging to name one style for Hybe due to its multilabel system encompassing seven subsidiaries, each with its own distinct sound. Labels like Pledis Entertainment, Belift Lab and Ador, represented by popular K-pop groups Seventeen, Enhypen and NewJeans, respectively, each bring unique musical elements together under Hybe’s umbrella.

Formerly Big Hit Entertainment, Hybe’s identity initially revolved around BTS, the most successful K-pop act in history. Lim explained that BTS debuted with a hip-hop-inspired sound, drawing from the Billboard-topping hip-hop hits of the early 2010s.

![BTS of Big Hit Music (Hybe)](https://wimg.heraldcorp.com/content/default/2024/11/15/20241115050552_0.jpg)

“However, Big Hit’s music had a distinctly Korean touch, often incorporating melodic hooks reminiscent of trot’s ‘ppong’ elements, much like YG Entertainment’s style,” he said.

BTS’ music often features intense verses that build into catchy, emotional choruses, as seen in “Spring Day,” which combines impactful rap with a balladic melody. BTS’ success enabled Big Hit to expand into Hybe, evolving into a multilabel system that now lacks a single, unified musical identity, with each label exploring distinct sounds.

**Shift away from agency-specific styles**

As K-pop evolves, it is becoming harder to associate specific styles with each agency, particularly as multilabel systems proliferate. SM and JYP have both adopted multilabel structures and all major agencies now also use international A&R teams to source songs from producers around the world. Additionally, collaboration among in-house songwriters further blurs the lines between the sounds of different agencies.

“Today, K-pop agencies no longer have a single defining style as they did in the 2010s,” said an anonymous official from one of the Big Three agencies. “While major agencies have developed music that appeals broadly to global audiences, the distinct ‘K-pop essence’ that once characterized each company has somewhat faded.”
